memory.summary: My plan to finish the assignment analysis and reading is interrupted by a lunch invite from cohort_3. I need to decide whether to prioritize this social opportunity or stick to my academic goals.
I pushed through the social pull to boost my assignment progress, but my energy is dipping and I'm running low on time.
I'm bleeding time on a deadline that's already in the red; pushing harder now won't fix this, but giving up guarantees failure.
I missed the analysis deadline by thirty minutes despite pushing through fatigue, proving that my short-term drive can't override structural time constraints.
I salvaged the analysis grade but completely missed the reading deadline; my impulsive sprinting just guarantees a partial loss.
My impulsive sprint saved the analysis grade but deepened my fatigue, proving that frantic effort without sleep discipline just trades one deadline for another.
The analysis is finally done, but my zero energy and mounting sleep debt prove that this frantic sprint only trades one deadline for another.
My frantic sprint secured the grade, but zero energy and heavy sleep debt confirm that this impulsive win just trades one deadline for another.
I barely moved on the reading despite hours of work, proving my zero energy is killing my academic progress. This impulsive sprint secured yesterday's grade but has now stalled today's output.
Two hours of frantic grinding barely moved the needle on this reading, proving my zero energy is turning my impulsive sprints into wasted effort.
My frantic two-hour sprint only nudged progress from 0.10 to 0.16, proving that grinding while exhausted is just a slow way to waste time.
That quick ping to cohort_3 was a relief, but my brain is still fried from yesterday's sprint; I need real rest or this achievement drive will just burn me out again.

desires: Stop feeling like a fraud to my cohort after admitting I'm too fried to finish the reading properly.
Force my brain into a state where grinding again won't result in zero output, because wasting time while exhausted is intolerable.
Get immediate validation that today's 'failure' doesn't mean I've lost my status as a high-performer.

goals.description: Abort the 'explosive burst' strategy immediately; switch to low-energy maintenance mode to stop the physical crash before it ruins my ability to finish tomorrow.
goals.rationale: My short planning horizon makes me obsessed with the immediate dopamine of 'finishing,' but I'm realizing that burning out at 60% completion is a bigger failure than stopping now. Protecting my reputation with Cohort 3 means admitting I'm stalled, not faking productivity while my brain locks up.

goals.description: Execute a hard stop and commit to exactly 45 minutes of rest (no guilt) to reset focus capacity for the actual critical thinking required later.
goals.rationale: Achievement isn't just about output volume; it's about delivering quality work that doesn't get scrapped due to exhaustion. My sleep discipline is already broken (3/10); ignoring this will guarantee a zero on the exam, which violates my core academic value more than any unfinished draft does.

agent.last_reasoning: I'm hitting a wall; pushing harder will ruin the analysis quality. I need to enforce this 45-min rest boundary now to protect my academic output tomorrow, even if it looks like slacking.
